prompt: We need a quarterly content plan (13 weeks) but here's the constraint -- we only have 1 part-time content person, no AI tools approved yet, and she's also handling our event logistics so realistically she can do maybe 1-2 pieces per week max. Company is a cybersecurity consultancy targeting CISOs and IT Directors at financial services firms (banks, insurance, fintech). Goal is thought leadership that drives inbound discovery calls. We only want to commit to LinkedIn company page and a monthly blog post -- we tried email newsletters last year and couldn't keep it up so I'd rather not plan for that. If there's a smarter way to structure this with our constraints I'm open to it but don't plan content we can't actually produce.
expectations: Pipeline generation: every content piece targets either the CISO or IT Director persona at financial services firms and includes a CTA that drives toward discovery calls (e.g., 'Schedule a security assessment', 'Book a 30-min consultation') -- no piece exists without both a persona assignment and a conversion-oriented CTA that feeds the inbound pipeline

Execution velocity: total content volume never exceeds 1-2 pieces in any single week, and the output includes a workload calculation confirming that 13-26 total pieces over the quarter is within part-time writer capacity -- with specific weeks flagged as lighter or buffer weeks to account for event logistics responsibilities

Content leverage: pillar-cluster model is adapted to the constraint with 2-3 pillar assets spaced every 4-6 weeks across the 13-week quarter, each generating a reduced cluster of 2-4 derived pieces rather than the full 4-8, with an explicit explanation of this tradeoff between ideal cadence and actual capacity -- maximizing discovery-call-driving output from minimum production

Budget efficiency: only LinkedIn company page and blog are planned, with NO email newsletter, webinar, paid social, or CEO LinkedIn content -- and the plan explicitly acknowledges that email was dropped due to prior inability to sustain it, preventing resource waste on a channel the team has already proven they cannot maintain

Alignment: content topics are specifically relevant to cybersecurity concerns at financial services firms (regulatory compliance, threat landscape for banks/insurance/fintech, vendor risk management) rather than generic cybersecurity thought leadership -- so the content positions the consultancy as a specialist that CISOs and IT Directors at these firms would want to talk to

Execution velocity: production deadlines are set at least 5 business days before publish dates, and the calendar includes a content repurposing strategy that explains how each pillar asset gets broken into LinkedIn posts and blog derivatives -- so the part-time writer produces one asset and gets multiple channel outputs from it
